title: Limonium cancellatum
taxonomy: Limonium cancellatum is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Plumbaginaceae. It is a part of the genus Limonium, which includes over 100 species of coastal plants. The plant is commonly known as the cancellated sea lavender. Limonium cancellatum is native to the Mediterranean region and is characterized by its pink or white flowers. The classification of Limonium cancellatum reflects its unique characteristics and its place within the broader plant kingdom.
